title: You should pool HTTP connections with HttpClientFactory
description: If a code path that creates and disposes of HttpClient objects is frequently used, then the following issues can occur: Under heavy load, there’s the risk of running out of available sockets, leading to SocketException errors. This is because each HttpClient instance uses a separate network connection, and there’s a limit to the number of connections that can be opened simultaneously. Note that even after you dispose of an HttpClient its sockets are not immediately freed up.
recommendation: Replace with the recommended alternative approach.

checks.message: HttpClient should be pooled using IHttpClientFactory or a static instance. Creating and disposing HttpClient per request can cause socket exhaustion.

logic.reasoning: Creating new HttpClient instances for each request and disposing them can lead to socket exhaustion under load. HttpClient should be reused via IHttpClientFactory (recommended) or a static instance.

should_trigger: foreach (var url in urls) { var client = new HttpClient(); }
should_not_trigger: private static readonly HttpClient _client = new HttpClient();
